Asparagus is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asparagaceae family, characterized by its edible shoots and medicinal properties. Asparagus has been traditionally used to support cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation, due to its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, and its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Asparagus include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Asparagus is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, to support overall health and well-being.

Asparagus devenishii is native to Australia and is often found in the wild. The classification of Asparagus devenishii includes its kingdom as Plantae and its phylum as Angiospermophyta. This plant is further classified under the class Liliopsida and order Asparagales.
